creator: tschweikle@gmail.com
text:
System boots only until network starts to initialize. At the second "ifconfig" it will hang indefinitely. Booting into single user, or booting without network makes it boot to login.

You can then configure networking using one "ifconfig em0 172.19.18.98 netmask 255.255.255.0".
Calling ifconfig a second time, even without any parameter given, will make it hang. ctrl-c wont stop this process, same for ctrl-z.

After defining a default route you may use networking as normal.

Hardware:
- Intel e1000, seen as em0

uname -a:
  FreeBSD  12.3-STABLE FreeBSD 12.3-STABLE stable/12-n234720-b02fa10699b2 FBSD12 amd64

Since virtualization with VMware or KVM-Qemu can emulate an Intel e1000 adapter you can observe the same with virtual machines running as guest within VMware Workstation or KVM-Qemu.
